goal: Restore guided colony intake so Aether synthesizes the best colony goal before init, asks for planning depth, clarification depth, verification strictness, and execution mode, persists those choices in colony state, gives explicit next-command guidance at every lifecycle step, and tells the user exactly when to clear context or resume across Claude, OpenCode, and Codex.

phases.name: Contract and gap mapping
phases.description: Lock the expected ant-process behavior against the current Go command surface.

tasks.goal: Compare the documented ant workflow with the current Codex command behavior
constraints: Keep the Go binary as the execution source of truth
Use Claude/OpenCode command specs as the external contract
success_criteria: The next implementation slices are dependency-ordered
The parity gaps are explicit

tasks.goal: Decide the observable ant-process outputs Codex must emit during each core command
constraints: Do not claim capabilities the Go binary does not actually perform
success_criteria: Each command has a concrete dispatch contract
Spawn tree and artifacts are part of the contract
The parity contract is explicit
The colony has an honest execution target

phases.name: Colonize orchestration
phases.description: Deliver surveyor-driven territory mapping that writes usable survey artifacts and spawn records.

tasks.goal: Implement the surveyor workflow for Codex colonize
constraints: Codex workflow docs describe ant-style worker orchestration, but `cmd/codex_workflow_cmds.go` still handles core lifecycle steps inline rather than dispatching specialized workers.
Follow the repository's existing structure and conventions
Keep changes scoped to the current goal
success_criteria: Survey artifacts are written
Surveyor spawns are recorded and completed

tasks.goal: Keep survey artifacts stable enough for planning to consume
constraints: Avoid polluting reports with cache or generated artifacts
hints: PATHOGENS.md should only mention real repo concerns
success_criteria: Noise from generated/cache files is excluded
Survey artifacts are reusable inputs for plan
Territory survey is reliable
Planning can trust the survey output

phases.name: Planning orchestration
phases.description: Add a scout plus route-setter planning pass that produces artifacts and a grounded phase plan.

tasks.goal: Implement a scout planning pass that summarizes survey findings into buildable guidance
constraints: Planning must still work when survey docs are missing
Scout output must be persisted as a planning artifact
success_criteria: Planning produces a scout artifact
The route-setter consumes scout output and survey context

tasks.goal: Generate a route-setter plan with task constraints, hints, and success criteria
constraints: The first phase must become ready
The saved plan must match the displayed plan
success_criteria: Plan generation is grounded in repo context
Spawn records show scout and route-setter activity
Plan generation is ant-driven
The colony has a grounded next phase

phases.name: Build orchestration
phases.description: Replace visual-only build dispatch with real worker execution slices and artifact handling.

tasks.goal: Define and implement the builder, watcher, and specialist work sequence for build
constraints: Recorded spawns must reflect real work performed
success_criteria: Build does more than set state
The spawn plan matches actual command behavior

tasks.goal: Persist build artifacts and phase context needed by continue
constraints: Continue must not rely on implied work that never happened
success_criteria: Continue can verify concrete outputs
Phase state stays consistent across reruns
Build behavior matches its visuals
Continue has real evidence to consume

phases.name: Continue orchestration
phases.description: Make continue perform the real verification and advancement work instead of only flipping statuses.

tasks.goal: Implement watcher-led verification and housekeeping before phase advancement
constraints: Completed phases must only advance after verification
Signal housekeeping should stay wired into continue finalize
success_criteria: Advance only happens after verification succeeds
Continue verifies actual artifacts

tasks.goal: Record the continue worker flow in state, spawn logs, and user-facing output
constraints: The Next Up block must stay valid for the resulting state
success_criteria: Continue output matches actual work
The next phase becomes ready when appropriate
Continue is no longer a pure state flip
Phase advancement is defensible

phases.name: End-to-end verification
phases.description: Run the colony from init through seal and prove the Codex ant process is now honest.

tasks.goal: Add tests that prove colonize, plan, build, and continue record real worker activity
constraints: Test the user path, not just helper functions
success_criteria: Core parity regressions are caught by tests
Spawn tree entries are asserted in tests

tasks.goal: Run a live colony loop and compare its outputs with the documented ant process
constraints: Call out any remaining parity gap explicitly
success_criteria: Remaining gaps are small and explicit
The live loop proves the implemented parity
The live colony loop is credible
The remaining parity gap is narrow and testable
